closure_effect.why: The sharper obstruction satisfies each issue's alternative closure criterion but does not choose a scheme representative. Numeric rows require a positive source RG trajectory or invariant plus a visibly declared conversion chart and certified transport. The stored GeV mass textures additionally require a common-scale dimensionless Yukawa conversion.

top.note: The current top coordinate is an extraction-codomain question and must not be folded into the u,d,s,c,b running-coordinate package.

external_chart_boundary.allowed_role: After OPH emits an RG-covariant physical trajectory or invariant, MSbar and a reporting scale may be declared as an external comparison chart.
external_chart_boundary.current_blocker: The physical trajectory/invariant is absent; merely naming a chart cannot supply its boundary data.

formal_quotient_obstruction.factorization_result: A map from the quotient X(P)/G_fin to a particular running coordinate does not exist without choosing a section of the finite-renormalization orbit.
formal_quotient_obstruction.reparametrization_group: G_fin: finite counterterms, RG-scale changes, and threshold conventions
formal_quotient_obstruction.source_invariance: X(P) and fully transformed physical amplitudes are constant on G_fin orbits
formal_quotient_obstruction.source_object: X(P): source-emitted physical quark data or physical amplitudes

heavy_threshold_obstruction.all_orders_statement: A compatible change of matching scale and decoupling coefficients leaves physical observables invariant.
heavy_threshold_obstruction.finite_order_statement: At finite order the residual matching-scale dependence is a truncation uncertainty, not an OPH prediction of a preferred threshold coordinate.

notes: This is a structural convention obstruction, not a numerical fit and not a target comparison.
A source theory should emit an RG-covariant trajectory, an invariant, or an operational observable.
MSbar and the quoted scales remain external coordinate metadata unless an additional section theorem is emitted.

physical_outputs_changed: threshold crossing locations induced by the mass spectrum
physical_rg_dynamics_nondefinability.same_source_completion_A: generic admissible normalized Yukawa boundary Y_u,Y_d at mu_source
physical_rg_dynamics_nondefinability.same_source_completion_B: independently rescaled generic admissible Yukawa boundary Y_u'=lambda_u*Y_u, Y_d'=lambda_d*Y_d
physical_rg_dynamics_nondefinability.statement: The current OPH source signature contains no normalized fermionic/Yukawa effective action, six-flavor matrix RG vector field, running Higgs expectation value, or completed heavy-threshold trajectory. Two completions can therefore agree on every emitted source object while carrying different renormalized Yukawa boundary data and different RGI quark masses. The physical RG trajectory itself, not merely its coordinate chart, is non-definable on the current corpus.

rg_covariant_source_output.invariant_formula: log(M_q) = log(m_q^S(mu)) + integral^a_S(mu) gamma_m^S(x)/beta^S(x) dx, with a declared integration normalization
rg_covariant_source_output.preferred_positive_theorem_target: Emit the RG-covariant quark trajectory (or its finite-renormalization equivalence class) and only then apply an external declared comparison chart.
required_payload: RG-covariant trajectory with an interval certificate
declared conversion chart used only after source emission

same_source_counterfamily.compensating_operator_change: C_q_epsilon(mu) = C_q(mu)/(1 + epsilon*a(mu)), so C_q_epsilon*m_q_epsilon = C_q*m_q
same_source_counterfamily.nonidentifiability_result: Distinct running-mass coordinates represent the same source and the same physical amplitudes. No target-free rule in the current corpus fixes the free orbit parameters.
same_source_counterfamily.scale_counterfamily: mu_prime = kappa*mu with kappa > 0, followed along the same RG trajectory

promotion_requirements: transport every running quark coordinate to one declared common scale
supply the common scheme and threshold-decoupling map
convert the separate top extraction coordinate to the common running scheme
emit the running electroweak vev v(mu_common) in the same scheme
divide the common-scale mass texture by v(mu_common)/sqrt(2)
certify dimensionless singular values and no-target provenance
stored_matrix_dimensional_audit.verdict: Matrices whose singular values are GeV-valued entries from mixed comparison charts are mass-texture matrices, not certified physical Yukawa matrices. Unitary flavor rotations do not repair the missing common-scale dimensionless normalization.

theorem_statement: Let X(P) denote source-emitted physical quark data and let G_fin be the group of finite renormalizations, RG-scale changes, and compatible threshold reparametrizations. Physical amplitudes and an RG-covariant trajectory factor through the quotient by G_fin, whereas a running coordinate m_q^S(mu) transforms nontrivially under G_fin. Therefore no G_fin-invariant source functional X(P) -> m_q^S(mu) can select a unique MSbar coordinate unless the source corpus supplies an additional scheme/readout section. The present corpus supplies no such section; its MSbar scale choices are declared comparison metadata.
